scan_output: Vulnerability mount_option_dev_shm_nosuid not found in single-rule scan (possibly fixed)

llm_verdict.message:
The finding `mount_option_dev_shm_nosuid` requires that the `nosuid` mount option be present for the `/dev/shm` filesystem to prevent the execution of setuid programs in shared memory.

Upon inspection of `/etc/fstab`, the entry for `/dev/shm` is:
`tmpfs /dev/shm tmpfs rw,seclabel,nosuid,nodev,inode64 0 0`

The `nosuid` option is already explicitly included in the configuration. Furthermore, verification of the active mount via `findmnt /dev/shm` confirms the option is currently in effect:
`TARGET   SOURCE FSTYPE OPTIONS`
`/dev/shm tmpfs  tmpfs  rw,nosuid,nodev,seclabel,inode64`

Since the `nosuid` option is already present and active, the system is already compliant with this security requirement. No remediation was necessary.
